act_title: *SYNOD OF THE DIOCESE OF WEST MALAYSIA (INCORPORATION) ACT 1971
content:
1.   This Act may be cited as the Synod of the Diocese of West
Malaysia (Incorporation) Act 1971.
Incorporation, etc.

act_title: *SYNOD OF THE DIOCESE OF WEST MALAYSIA (INCORPORATION) ACT 1971
content:
2.   (1)  The Synod of the Diocese of West Malaysia, and its
6                                 Laws of Malaysia
ACT 36
successors shall be a body corporate and shall have perpetual
succession under the name “The Synod of the Diocese of West
Malaysia” hereinafter called the Corporation and shall be the
governing body of the Anglican Church in Peninsular Malaysia.
(2) The Corporation may enter into contracts, and may acquire,
purchase, take, hold and enjoy movable and immovable property of
every description and may convey, transfer, assign, surrender and yield
up, charge, mortgage, demise, reassign or otherwise dispose of, or deal
with, any movable or immovable property vested in the Corporation
and may lend or borrow money upon such terms as to the Standing
Committee of the Corporation, hereafter referred to as the Standing
Committee, may seem fit, and the minutes of the Standing Committee
shall be sufficient evidence of such terms.
(3)
The Corporation may sue and be sued in respect of its property
and otherwise in all courts of justice and in all matters and suits.
Common seal of Corporation, etc.

act_title: *SYNOD OF THE DIOCESE OF WEST MALAYSIA (INCORPORATION) ACT 1971
content:
3. (1) The Corporation shall have a common seal, and such seal may
from time to time be broken, changed, altered and made anew as to the
Standing Committee seems fit.
(2)
The seal of the Corporation shall not be affixed to any deed,
document or other instrument except by authority of a resolution of the
Standing Committee and in the presence of two members of the
Standing Committee one of whom shall be the Bishop of the Diocese
of West Malaysia or the Vicar-General appointed by the Bishop or by
the Archbishop of the Province of the Anglican Church in South-east
Asia during a vacancy in the See of West Malaysia or the Chancellor,
Treasurer, or Secretary of the Corporation.
(3)  All deeds, documents and other instruments requiring the seal
of the Corporation shall be sealed with the seal of the Corporation
and shall also be signed by the two witnesses referred to in
subsection (2).
Synod of the Diocese of West Malaysia
(Incorporation)
7
(4)   Such signing shall be sufficient evidence that the said seal was
duly and properly affixed and that the said seal is the lawful seal of the
Corporation.
(5)  A certificate purporting to be signed by the Bishop of the
Diocese of West Malaysia or in his absence the Vicar-General
appointed by the Bishop or by the Archbishop of the Province of the
Anglican Church in South-east Asia during a vacancy in the See of
West Malaysia shall be sufficient evidence as to the persons who from
time to time are members of the Standing Committee and who are the
Chancellor, Treasurer and Secretary of the Corporation.
Transfer of certain properties

act_title: *SYNOD OF THE DIOCESE OF WEST MALAYSIA (INCORPORATION) ACT 1971
content:
Transfer of certain properties
4. (1) The Secretary of the Synod of the Diocese of Singapore as
incorporated under the Synod of the Diocese of Singapore Ordinance
1958 in whom is vested all movable and immovable property situated in
Peninsular Malaysia belonging to the Corporation immediately before the
commencement of this Act is hereby divested of all such movable and
immovable property and all estates, rights and interests therein.
(2)  All movable and immovable property referred to in subsection (1)
and all estates, rights and interests therein are hereby vested in the
Corporation for the respective estates, rights and interests for which
the same are held and the Corporation shall have full power to deal
with the said property as provided in subsection 2(2).
(3)  The appropriate Land Administrator or Registrar of Titles, as
the case may be, shall substitute the name of the Corporation in place
of that of the registered holder in the case of any registered interest in
immovable property in Peninsular Malaysia formerly vested in the
Secretary of the Synod of the Diocese of Singapore.
Repeal
